title: Integrate forest travel and world-map scene into the live app
summary: This packet hands the main agent a concrete travel scaffold: a second biome, overworld location data, map movement helpers, a map renderer, and a staged doorway transition planner. The live integration work is now to connect those modules into the existing runtime without regressing the beach slice.

execution_order.goal: Generalize the current biome renderer so beach and forest can each draw their own terrain and atmosphere.
execution_order.why_now: The live render path is still beach-shaped, so travel integration will look wrong until biome rendering becomes data-aware.

execution_order.goal: Add a scene state machine for biome play, world map, and travel transitions.
execution_order.why_now: World-map movement and door transitions should not be squeezed into the current overlay-only state model.

execution_order.goal: Place biome doors using the anchors in src/content/world-map.ts and trigger travel out of the biome scene.
execution_order.why_now: Door anchors are the bridge between side-view exploration and the overworld map.

execution_order.goal: Wire world-map focus, walking, and rendering into the new world-map scene.
execution_order.why_now: The overworld helpers are already test-covered and can be connected once scene ownership is clear.

execution_order.goal: Drive the bridge animation with createDoorTransitionPlan() and sampleDoorTransition().
execution_order.why_now: The transition module already defines the staged feel the user asked for: exit door, reveal map, walk route, enter door, reveal destination.

execution_order.goal: Persist biome travel state through the existing save model and verify discoveries still work across ecosystems.
execution_order.why_now: Travel should expand the game, not reset or fragment the journal loop.

guardrails: Do not regress the current beach slice while integrating the forest.
Keep science accuracy and kid readability intact.
Treat the world-map modules as the source of truth for travel routes and door anchors.
Avoid burying map logic and transition sampling directly inside large render functions when helper modules already exist.
Preserve deterministic hooks and save behavior.
validation: travel from beach to forest and back in the live app
confirm discoveries and journal entries persist across biome changes
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, and this packet. Use the scaffolded forest biome, world-map content, map renderer, and door-transition planner to integrate travel into the live runtime. Keep the beach slice stable while making forest travel feel like a readable doorway-to-map-to-door journey.
